questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
Paragraph: A wheel of a car is made up of two parts (1)the central metal rim, and (2)the rubber tyre. The width of the tyre $W=16.5 \mathrm{~cm}$ and height $h=10.7 \mathrm{~cm}$. The rim overlaps the tyre. The total weight of the car is 1500 kg distributed evenly. The tyres are inflated with air to a pressure $2.0 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{cm}^2$. The density of air at pressure of $1.0 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ and at room temperature equals 1.29 g /litre. The outer diameter of the tyre is 55.4 cm and that of the rim is 40 cm . Ignore the thickness of rubber and use the dimensions given here. Note that the units mentioned above are conventional units used in everyday life.
Question: When five persons occupy the seats $L$ increases by 2.5 cm . The average weight of a person is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A heavy metal block is dragged along a rough horizontal surface at a constant speed of $20 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$. The coefficient of friction between the block and the surface is 0.6 . The block is made of a material whose specific heat is $0.1 \mathrm{cal} / \mathrm{g}-{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and absorbs $25 \%$ of heat generated due to friction. If the block is dragged for 10 min , the rise in temperature of the block is about $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right)$

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A body of mass 1.0 kg moves in X-Y plane under the influence of a conservative force. Its potential energy is given by $\mathrm{U}=2 x+3 y$ where $(x, y)$ denote the coordinates of the body. The body is at rest at $(2,-4)$ initially. All the quantities have SI units. Therefore, the body
options: has a speed of $2 \sqrt{13} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ at time $t=2 \mathrm{~s}$.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A particle of mass $m$ is located in a one dimensional potential field $U(x)=U_0(1-\cos a x)$; $\mathrm{U}_0$ and a are constants. Which of the following statement/s is/are correct?
options: The particle will execute Simple Harmonic Motion for small displacements.
The time period of small oscillations is $\frac{2 \pi}{a} \sqrt{\frac{m}{U_0}}$
The angular frequency for small oscillations is $\omega=a \sqrt{\frac{U_0}{m}}$
